title: Legal Source Manifest (internal, v1)
description: INTERNAL, NON-CANONICAL manifest for one captured legal/official source in the M3 legal corpus (task M3-T001, D-002 first-wave lane 1). It records the provenance, corpus version, and raw-capture hash that make a source traceable and precedence-resolvable per docs/SOURCE_AUTHORITY_POLICY.md. SCOPE DISCIPLINE: this is an internal manifest, deliberately NOT one of the canonical cross-tier contracts (property_profile / rule_evaluation / coverage_status / scenario / source_fact / analysis_state_transition). It is NOT in packages/contracts/scripts SCHEMA_FILES and is NOT a TypeScript typegen target, so adding it changes no generated artifact. It must NOT be promoted into a canonical cross-tier contract without a justified cross-tier consumer and a controlled contract change (AS-11e). DETERMINISM: this schema is hand-authored and static (no generator); its $id and x-contract-version are fixed constants, so it is byte-identical across regeneration (AS-11d). CHANGE DISCIPLINE: changes are additive with an x-contract-version bump; older manifests remain valid. This manifest describes a SOURCE's capture/verification disposition; it is distinct from the per-evaluation runtime status vocabulary in docs/LEGAL_CORPUS_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md and from the document evidence-state machine in M3-T003.

manifest_version.description: Manifest schema version this record conforms to. Fixed constant for v1; a schema change bumps this additively.

manifest_id.description: Stable identifier of this manifest record (e.g. 'lsm:zr:2026-05-20:<short-hash>'). Construction is documented per corpus task; must be unique within the corpus.

provenance.description: Source-intrinsic provenance. REQUIRED as a whole; a manifest with no provenance block is invalid (AS-11c). Feeds precedence resolution (SOURCE_AUTHORITY_POLICY.md §3).

source_id.description: Source-registry identifier of the official source (docs/SOURCE_ACCESS_REGISTRY.md). Third-party/reference sources (tier 5/6) use a clearly non-official id and are NEVER controlling provenance.

publisher.description: Publishing body, e.g. 'NYC DCP', 'NYC DOB', 'NYC Council'. For a third-party reference this is the vendor (e.g. 'UpCodes, Inc.') and the tier must be 5.

provenance_tier.description: Provenance CLASSIFICATION 1-7 per SOURCE_AUTHORITY_POLICY.md §2. This is a provenance class, NOT a conflict-resolution rank: a tier number never decides a precedence conflict (that is §3). Tier 5 (third-party) / tier 6 (client) are never controlling provenance; tier 7 (AI) has no authority.

legal_status.description: Legal/operative status used by precedence resolution (SOURCE_AUTHORITY_POLICY.md §3.1 factors 2-4). 'reference_only' MUST be used for tier-5 third-party references; 'ai_proposed' for tier-7 AI proposals (no authority); 'factual' for tier-4 datasets.

jurisdiction.description: Optional. Governing jurisdiction/scope of the source (e.g. 'NYC', 'NYS'); used by precedence factor 1 (jurisdiction). Null when not applicable/known.

scope.description: Precedence factor 5 (scope specificity). 'project_specific' instruments (variance/special permit/restrictive declaration/ZLDA) may control the affected property within their granted scope, and are NEVER generalized to another property.

canonical_url.description: Canonical official URL the source was retrieved from. For sources behind the nyc.gov bot wall or otherwise unresolved, the manifest status is 'blocked_at_g1' until the URL is verified (AS-6).

access_mode.description: Machine token for the registry access-mode taxonomy (docs/SOURCE_ACCESS_REGISTRY.md governance rule 4). 'reference_only' is used for tier-5/6 cross-check sources that add no runtime dependency.

retrieved_at.description: Timestamp at which the raw source was retrieved (RFC 3339).

effective_date.description: Effective date where the source publishes one (precedence factor 3). Key present and explicitly null when none is published, so absence is a visible statement, never a silent omission or a guess.

as_of_date.description: Currency/'as-of' date carried by the source (e.g. the ZR homepage banner date). Null when none is published.

supersedes.description: Optional manifest_ids this source supersedes (precedence factor 4). AI may PROPOSE supersession edges; they are validated downstream and never approved by AI.

superseded_by.description: Optional manifest_id that supersedes this source, or null.

corpus_version.description: REQUIRED corpus version label for this capture (AS-11c). Deterministic label grounded in an official signal, e.g. 'zr-banner-2026-05-20' or a snapshot/edition id. A manifest with no corpus_version is invalid.

raw_capture.description: Immutable raw-capture facts. REQUIRED (AS-11c). The raw hash is content-addressable; the manifest never carries the raw bytes (bulk bytes live in approved object storage per M3-T002).

raw_sha256.description: REQUIRED content hash of the exact raw captured bytes, 'sha256:' + 64 lowercase hex (AS-11c). A manifest with no raw hash, or a malformed hash, is invalid.

media_type.description: IANA media type of the raw capture, e.g. 'text/html', 'application/pdf'.

byte_size.description: Exact byte length of the raw capture.

storage_ref.description: Optional content-addressable reference into approved object storage (M3-T002 append-only interface); null while durable storage is blocked (B-001).

status.description: SOURCE capture/verification disposition (NOT a rule-evaluation outcome). 'to_verify_at_g1' is a proposal marker; 'blocked_at_g1' is used when identity/endpoint/access/terms/URL is unresolved (AS-6) — never 'verified_available' on faith. Distinct from the per-evaluation vocabulary in LEGAL_CORPUS_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md §2.

cross_check_only.description: True for tier-5/6 sources recorded for human cross-check only (UpCodes/ZoLa). When true, this source is NEVER controlling provenance and adds no runtime dependency (NC-3).

notes.description: Optional free-text provenance note; never a substitute for a required field.

non_empty_string.description: String that must not be empty.

date_time.description: RFC 3339 timestamp. Pattern enforced so validation behaves identically with or without a format-aware validator (mirrors common.schema.json).

digest_sha256.description: 'sha256:' + lowercase-hex SHA-256, mirroring common.schema.json digest_sha256. For raw_capture this digests the exact raw bytes (content-addressable), not a canonical JSON form.
